wine_bars_late.name: Enoteca Picone
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Over 2,000 labels from Sicily and Italy's finest producers; over 30 by-the-glass selections daily. Strong on Etna, Cerasuolo di Vittoria and aged Marsala.
wine_bars_late.tip: Enoteca Picone is Palermo's institution for serious wine retail and tasting. The standing bar at the back is where you'll find Sicilian sommeliers on their days off.
wine_bars_late.description: Founded in 1946 on Via Marconi, Enoteca Picone is Palermo's most historically significant wine shop and tasting bar. Over 2,000 labels, strong focus on Sicilian DOC wines and aged vintages.

wine_bars_late.name: Bocum
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Around 200 labels focused on Etna DOC and natural Sicilian producers; 20-plus by the glass, rotating weekly
wine_bars_late.tip: Bocum's late closing makes it Catania's go-to after-dinner Etna wine destination. The by-the-glass list changes weekly and tends toward small-producer natural Etna DOC.
wine_bars_late.description: Catania natural-wine bar in Piazza San Placido, with a rotating list of Etna DOC small-producers and broader Sicilian and Italian natural wines. Late closing attracts the post-dinner wine crowd.

wine_bars_late.name: Vino e Olio
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Curated selection of Sicilian and Italian natural wines, 25 by-the-glass; Etna, Cerasuolo di Vittoria and Sicilia DOC whites feature prominently
wine_bars_late.tip: Ortigia's most serious wine bar, set in the baroque island centro storico. Late evening crowds spill onto the street in summer; arrive early for a table.
wine_bars_late.description: Ortigia wine bar with a natural and artisanal focus. Strong Sicilian selection by the glass in the baroque setting of historic Syracuse.

wine_bars_late.name: Il Barocco
wine_bars_late.wine_program: All-Sicilian list of 150-plus bottles with a focus on Val di Noto Nero d'Avola and Cerasuolo di Vittoria; 20 by the glass
wine_bars_late.tip: The best place in Noto to taste Nero d'Avola from Avola and Vittoria producers side-by-side in a single evening.
wine_bars_late.description: Noto wine bar in the UNESCO-listed baroque city, with an all-Sicilian list anchored in Val di Noto Nero d'Avola and Cerasuolo di Vittoria.

wine_bars_late.name: Osteria del Vespri
wine_bars_late.address: Piazza Croce dei Vespri 6, 90133 Palermo, Italy
wine_bars_late.wine_program: 250-plus label Italian list with the strongest Sicily section outside Enoteca Picone; 30 by-the-glass
wine_bars_late.tip: The bar at the back serves the same list as the restaurant but without the full-menu commitment. Best late-evening stop for a glass of aged Marsala or Etna Rosso Riserva.
wine_bars_late.description: Palermo's most refined restaurant-wine-bar in a 19th-century palazzo in the Kalsa neighbourhood. Serious cellar of aged Sicilian and Italian wines; by-the-glass selection changes weekly.

wine_bars_late.name: Enoteca Terra e Vino
wine_bars_late.wine_program: All-Sicilian wine list of 200-plus labels; 20 by the glass; strong on Nero d'Avola, Grillo and Agrigento-province producers
wine_bars_late.tip: Located on Agrigento's main historic street, the best stopping point for a glass of Valle dei Templi-area Grillo or Nero d'Avola before or after visiting the temples.
wine_bars_late.description: Agrigento wine bar on Via Atenea with an all-Sicilian list focused on the Agrigento province's Grillo and Nero d'Avola producers. Late evenings and weekend wine events.

wine_bars_late.name: L'Enoteca degli Archi
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Palermo Piazza Marina wine bar with 180 Sicilian labels; 18 by the glass; outdoor seating on the archway piazza late into the night
wine_bars_late.tip: Piazza Marina is one of Palermo's most atmospheric evening squares. The all-Sicilian by-the-glass list is a useful shortcut to the island's DOC geography in one sitting.
wine_bars_late.description: Palermo wine bar on Piazza Marina in the historic Piazza Marina neighbourhood, with late outdoor seating and an all-Sicilian wine list by the glass.

listening_bars.name: Vineria Modica
listening_bars.wine_program: Small vinyl-and-wine bar with a rotating natural Sicilian selection; 15 by the glass
listening_bars.tip: Modica's bohemian wine bar with vinyl jazz and natural Sicilian wines. A night-owl favourite on Corso Umberto with late closing and a compact but personal selection.
listening_bars.description: Small vinyl-and-wine bar on Modica's main corso, combining natural Sicilian wines with vinyl music. The Cerasuolo di Vittoria pours are a highlight.

listening_bars.name: Il Cantuccio
listening_bars.wine_program: Laid-back Ortigia bar with vinyl soundtrack and Sicilian natural wine; 12 by the glass from small producers
listening_bars.tip: The most relaxed late-night wine venue on Ortigia island with a curated vinyl collection and rotating producer-by-producer Sicilian pours.
listening_bars.description: Casual Ortigia listening bar on Via dei Mille, combining vinyl music with Sicilian natural wines and small-producer pours.

listening_bars.name: Cantina Siciliana
listening_bars.wine_program: Trapani wine bar with a laid-back atmosphere, local musicians performing Thursday to Saturday and a 180-label Sicilian list
listening_bars.tip: Thursday-Saturday live music with Sicilian folk and jazz; arrive before 22:00 for a table. The Marsala and Grillo pours are the best in central Trapani.
listening_bars.description: Historic Trapani wine bar and restaurant in the Jewish quarter, with Sicilian music evenings and a strong focus on Marsala and Trapani-province wines.

candle_lit.name: La Bottega del Vino
candle_lit.wine_program: Intimate low-lit Catania wine bar with 200-plus bottles focused on Etna DOC and Sicilian producers; 20 by the glass in a candlelit back room
candle_lit.tip: The most atmospheric wine bar in central Catania for an Etna-focused evening. The cellar room has bare stone and candlelight; book ahead in summer.
candle_lit.description: Intimate candlelit Catania wine bar focused on Etna DOC and Sicilian artisan producers. Strong by-the-glass selection from small estates.

candle_lit.name: Enoteca il Consorzio
candle_lit.wine_program: Candlelit Palermo enoteca with 300-plus labels and intimate seating; specialises in Sicilian artisan producers and aged vintages
candle_lit.tip: One of Palermo's most atmospheric wine spaces. The owner knows every producer personally; ask for a vertical of an aged Nero d'Avola from Gulfi to experience the grape's development arc.
candle_lit.description: Intimate Palermo enoteca with candlelit setting, a deep Sicilian cellar and personal service. Hosts occasional producer evenings and vintage vertical tastings.

candle_lit.name: Enoteca del Duomo
candle_lit.wine_program: Taormina's most refined wine bar, with Etna DOC and Sicilian premium selection; 25 by the glass; terrace with Etna view
candle_lit.tip: The outdoor terrace at the Piazza del Duomo is Taormina's most atmospheric wine-tasting spot; the view of the illuminated Baroque cathedral with Etna behind it is unmatched on the island.
candle_lit.description: Taormina wine bar on the famous Piazza del Duomo, with a terrace view of the Baroque cathedral and Mount Etna. Focused Etna DOC selection by the glass.

candle_lit.name: Cantina del Re
candle_lit.wine_program: Candlelit Ragusa Ibla wine bar with 180 Sicilian labels; strong on Cerasuolo di Vittoria and natural producers from the Ragusa-Vittoria area
candle_lit.tip: The most intimate late-night spot in Ragusa Ibla's baroque piazza, with candles, arched stone ceilings and a list focused on the DOCG zone around Vittoria.
candle_lit.description: Atmospheric Ragusa Ibla wine bar in a historic stone cellar, focused on Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG and the natural wine producers of the Ragusa area.

candle_lit.name: Enoteca I Vespri
candle_lit.address: Via San Francesco di Paola 1, 91100 Trapani, Italy
candle_lit.wine_program: Low-lit Trapani enoteca with 200 Sicilian labels; 18 by the glass; Marsala DOC vertical and Grillo pours are the house anchors
candle_lit.tip: The Marsala vertical tasting here covers five years of Superiore Riserva from two producers side by side: the best value fortified-wine education in western Sicily.
candle_lit.description: Candlelit Trapani wine bar in the historic centro storico with Marsala DOC and Grillo as the centrepiece; a quieter counterpart to the livelier seafront bars.

verified.address_quoted: Via San Francesco di Paola 1, Trapani

candle_lit.name: La Cantina del Vino
candle_lit.wine_program: Intimate Noto wine cellar with stone-vaulted interior; 15 by-the-glass selections focused on Val di Noto Nero d'Avola and orange wine
candle_lit.tip: The stone cellar setting makes this Noto's most atmospheric late-evening pour; ask for the orange Zibibbo by a small-producer if the season's allocation has arrived.
candle_lit.description: Atmospheric Noto wine bar in a historic vaulted cellar, focused on Val di Noto Nero d'Avola, Frappato and orange wine. Intimate candlelit atmosphere on the main corso.

lounges.name: Vinodromo
lounges.address: Via Principe di Villafranca 50, 90141 Palermo, Italy
lounges.wine_program: Modern Palermo lounge-bar with a 300-plus Italian wine list; 25 by the glass; weekly producer evenings
lounges.tip: Vinodromo holds weekly producer tastings with Sicilian winemakers; check their social media for the calendar. The lounge setting attracts a younger Palermo professional crowd than the older enoteche.
lounges.description: Contemporary Palermo wine lounge in the Liberta neighbourhood with a wide Italian and Sicilian list and regular producer evening events.

lounges.name: Vino e Affini
lounges.wine_program: Modern Catania wine lounge with 250 Italian labels; 20 by the glass rotating weekly; Etna DOC and Sicilia DOC prominently featured
lounges.tip: One of Catania's newer and more design-led wine spaces; the courtyard is the main attraction for warm evenings. Etna Rosso by the glass with a charcuterie board is the standard order.
lounges.description: Contemporary Catania wine lounge in the San Berillo neighbourhood with a modern aesthetic and rotating weekly by-the-glass list focused on Etna and Sicilian producers.

lounges.name: La Bussola Lounge, Taormina
lounges.wine_program: Hotel terrace lounge bar with cocktail-and-wine programme; 20 Sicilian and Italian selections by the glass; Etna DOC featured prominently
lounges.tip: Terrace lounge with one of the best views in Taormina: the Ionian sea on one side, Etna on the other. The Etna Rosso pours are surprisingly good for a hotel bar.
lounges.description: Taormina hotel terrace lounge bar with panoramic Etna and Ionian coast views, offering Sicilian wine by the glass alongside classic cocktails.

fortified_specialists.name: Cantine Florio Enoteca Bar
fortified_specialists.wine_program: The in-house enoteca bar at the Florio bagli offers the full Marsala range by the glass: Fine, Superiore, Superiore Riserva, Vergine and Vergine Stravecchio
fortified_specialists.tip: Ask the bar staff for a Vergine Stravecchio aged 15-plus years alongside the Fine to experience the full complexity arc of Marsala in a single sitting.
fortified_specialists.description: The tasting bar at the historic Florio bagli on the Marsala seafront: the definitive venue for a structured Marsala by-the-glass experience, from the lightest Fine to 20-year Vergine Stravecchio.

fortified_specialists.name: Pellegrino Tasting Room
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Pellegrino's in-house tasting room offers the full range of Marsala DOC styles alongside the producer's dry Sicilian wines; 8 by the glass
fortified_specialists.tip: Pellegrino's Marsala Superiore Riserva and Vergine are the standouts for quality; try the Zibibbo dessert wine alongside the dry Grillo for a full taste of the Pellegrino range.
fortified_specialists.description: The tasting room at Carlo Pellegrino's Marsala winery, one of the city's founding bagli, offering structured Marsala pours from Fine through Vergine and vintage dessert wines.

fortified_specialists.name: Marco de Bartoli Estate Tasting Room
fortified_specialists.wine_program: The de Bartoli family's estate tasting room pours the full range including Vecchio Samperi (unfortified oxidative), Marsala Superiore and the Josephine Doree single-cru
fortified_specialists.tip: Vecchio Samperi is the signature pour: unfortified, perpetual-blend oxidative Grillo. It technically cannot be sold as Marsala (no added spirit) but it is conceptually the most intellectually honest version of what Marsala could be.
fortified_specialists.description: The Marco de Bartoli estate in Petrosino outside Marsala offers the most intellectually rigorous fortified and oxidative wine tasting in western Sicily, anchored by the Vecchio Samperi.

fortified_specialists.name: Vino e Convivio
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Marsala-focused wine bar with independent by-the-glass selection across producers: Florio, Pellegrino, de Bartoli, Donnafugata; 15 by the glass
fortified_specialists.tip: The only Marsala bar where you can taste all the major producers' Vergine and Superiore styles side by side in one evening without a cellar tour booking.
fortified_specialists.description: Independent Marsala wine bar on Via XI Maggio with a cross-producer Marsala by-the-glass selection: the most comprehensive single-stop for tasting the DOC typology.

late_tastings.name: Casa del Vino Siciliano
late_tastings.wine_program: All-Sicilian selection of 250 labels; 20 by the glass rotating weekly; producer guest appearances on Friday evenings
late_tastings.tip: Friday late tastings with invited Etna and Vittoria producers are the best regular event on the Catania wine calendar. The 23:00-01:00 sessions are uniquely Sicilian in their informality.
late_tastings.description: Catania's dedicated Sicilian wine bar on Via Landolina with late closing, Friday producer evenings and an all-island list. The closest Catania equivalent to a natural wine bar in Rome or Milan.

late_tastings.name: Ballaro 25
late_tastings.wine_program: Natural-wine focused late-night bar in the Ballaro market area; 15 small-producer Sicilian bottles by the glass on a chalk-board menu
late_tastings.tip: The latest-closing dedicated wine bar in Palermo's market district. The list changes daily based on open bottles; all natural, mostly Sicilian with the occasional biodynamic Campanian or Lazio pour.
late_tastings.description: Natural wine bar in Palermo's Ballaro market neighbourhood with a very late closing and a chalk-board daily list. Known as the post-midnight drinking spot for Palermo's wine trade.

late_tastings.name: Enoteca Teatro Greco
late_tastings.wine_program: Taormina evening wine bar by the Greek theatre; 20 Sicilian and Italian selections by the glass; strong on Etna and Nerello Mascalese
late_tastings.tip: Positioned one street from the entrance to the Greek Theatre; the perfect pre- or post-theatre venue for a glass of Etna Rosso. Outdoor terrace fills up on performance nights.
late_tastings.description: Taormina wine bar adjacent to the ancient Greek Theatre, serving Etna DOC and Sicilian selections by the glass in the most historically charged location on the island.

late_tastings.name: Enoteca del Porto
late_tastings.wine_program: Palermo late-evening wine bar at the old marina; 22 Sicilian labels by the glass including producer tasting flights on Saturday evenings
late_tastings.tip: Saturday evening producer flights focus on one region each week: Etna, Vittoria, Marsala or Alcamo. The waterfront terrace stays open late through October.
late_tastings.description: Palermo marina wine bar on Via Francesco Crispi with late Saturday producer tasting flights and an all-island by-the-glass selection. One of the city's few bars offering structured tasting formats after midnight.

sparkling_rooms.name: Bulle Palermo
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Palermo's dedicated sparkling wine bar with Sicilian metodo classico alongside Champagne, Prosecco and Franciacorta; 20 bubbles by the glass
sparkling_rooms.tip: Sicily's own metodo classico producers (Baglio di Pianetto, Cusumano) are featured alongside French Champagne; a useful introduction to the island's emerging sparkling-wine production.
sparkling_rooms.description: Palermo sparkling wine bar focused on Sicilian metodo classico, Champagne and Italian spumante selections. One of the island's few dedicated sparkling-focused late-evening venues.

sparkling_rooms.name: Frizzante Catania
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Catania sparkling bar featuring Sicilian metodo classico, Etna white petnat and selected Italian and French bubbles; 18 by the glass
sparkling_rooms.tip: The Etna petnat pours are the highlight: low-intervention sparkling Nerello Mascalese or Carricante in bottle-fermented pet-nat style is a uniquely Sicilian drinking experience.
sparkling_rooms.description: Catania sparkling wine bar in Piazza Bellini specialising in Sicilian petnat, metodo classico and Italian bubbles alongside French Champagne.

sparkling_rooms.name: Bollicine Wine Bar
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Taormina sparkling wine bar on the main corso with 20 by-the-glass selections: Sicilian metodo classico (Firriato, Baglio di Pianetto), Prosecco, Cava and Champagne
sparkling_rooms.tip: Best placed for a pre-dinner glass on the passeggiata; the Sicilian metodo classico from Firriato is consistently one of the best-value islands bubbles on the island and available here by the glass.
sparkling_rooms.description: Taormina corso sparkling wine bar with a strong Sicilian metodo classico and Italian bubbles programme alongside French Champagne. Street-side tables make it a natural passeggiata stop.
